entry.title: Improve Build Apps UIs to Allow Keyboard Click / Control Focus
entry.content:
I was doing this great tutorial by my colleagues @Rekha_DR and @shrinivasan_neelamegam about creating a chatbot using Build Apps and AI Core, a topic they delivered in the SAP TechEd developer keynote in 2024: https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2024-developer-keynote/blob/main/topics/DEV100A-build-chatbot-apps-aicore/README.md

It worked perfectly. 

The tutorial let you create a chatbot where you typed a prompt, got an answer, and then could send a new prompt, like a regular chatbot. (By the way, one of the clever designs of theirs was that the context of the conversation was always sent back to the AI Core with every subsequent prompt.)

I thought what would make the app even more helpful would be:

  • To be able to press Enter after typing in each prompt.
  • To place the cursor automatically in the prompt input box so the user could just start typing the next prompt.

 

Allow Enter Instead of Button Click

To do this, you first want to create a custom event for the logic that you want connected to the button click / Enter press.

Drag a Receive Event flow function onto the canvas.

Set the Event Source to Fired from "Trigger Event", and give the event a name, like SendChat.

Attach your logic (in this case the calls to AI Core) to the event.

Now all that's left is to trigger this event when the user clicks the button, or presses Enter. 

For the button click, select the button click, drag a Trigger Event flow function, and connect it to your Component Tap event for the button.

Configure the Trigger Event flow function by selecting your new event to be triggered.

To enable the logic to be triggered by pressing Enter, highlight your input box, and create the following logic on the input box's onKeyPress event.

To the onKeyPress event, add an If Condition flow function with the following formula:

outputs["Receive event"].eventValue == "Enter"

And then to the top output add a Trigger Event, configured the same as for the button click logic above.

 

Place Cursor in Input Box

Every time I send a prompt, I want the cursor to return to the input box so I can type in the next prompt.

Install the Focus Input flow function from the Marketplace.

After the logic for sending the prompt and receiving the answer from AI Core, add this flow function and specify the input box.

😽

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content:
@Cocquerel The short answer is this is a function of AI Core, and I am not an expert on the intricacies of how AI Core creates a pipeline and handles the data from the document. ARe you saying you want the LLM to provide an answer to a prompt, but then a type of footnote linking to the document it took the answer from?

 @noravonthenen maybe you know how this could be done?

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content: @chen_zhao02 Thanks for pointing out. Fixed.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content:
@Cocquerel Firstly, the page in discovery center is confusing to me since it seems to say a Record costs 0.005 of an AI Unit, and an AI Unit costs 7 euros, but then it says cost of a record is 7 euros (I would have thought it was 3.5 cents in euros).

It looks like the official page for AI pricing is here: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/pricing.html

On this page it talks a little bit more about pricing and records: https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/integrating-joule-with-sap/metering-and-pricing?locale=en-US

It's not clear to me if I upload a document that is 1KB would I still pay for 1 record, or whether 45MB can count for several documents.

 I am not an expert on pricing, sorry.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content: @Cocquerel As far as I know, at the moment, you cannot create a pipeline in AI Core and then use it in Joule Studio. But I am told they are working on that.

entry.title: 🇨🇭 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, March 17, 2026 from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Risch-Rotkeuz, Switzerland (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: GAMBIT Consulting AG, Suurstoffi 22, 6343 Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland (8th floor)

  • The event is kindly being hosted by GAMBIT – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 22 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content: @chul_lee2 Sorry for the delay in responding. I have also found that sometimes Joule Studio/Joule does not recognize the document content immediately. Sometimes I need to refresh, sometimes remove the document tool and re-enter ... not sure when it happens, why is happens, or exactly how to fix. I will ask development if there are any clues.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇹 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Milan, Italy)
entry.content: Happy to put you on a wait list, sometimes spots open up.

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content: Happy to join, but I may drink wine instead of beer, or even whiskey. I hope that is OK..

entry.title: 🇩🇪 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Herne, Germany)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, April 14, 2026 from 09:00 - 15:00 local time in Herne, Germany (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: rku.it GmbH, Westring 303, Herne, Germany

  • The event is kindly being hosted by rku – THANK YOU!
  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocates @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: 🇩🇪 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Bonn, Germany)
entry.content:
IMPORTANT: We have reached our capacity for the event. You can still register and you will put on a waitlist, and we will notify you a few days before if space opens up, which it often does.

This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, April 16, 2026 from 09:00 - 15:00 local time in Bonn, Germany (CEST). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: DHL Post Tower, Charles-de-Gaulle-Str. 20, 53113 Bonn, Germany

  • Building/Room: Post Tower/02.230 Washington
  • Address (Entrance): Platz der Deutschen Post, 53113 Bonn, Germany
  • Address (Parking): Charles-de-Gaulle-Str. 20, 53113 Bonn, Germany
  • Upon arrival, please contact the reception desk at the Post Tower for accreditation.

More information:

  • The event is kindly being hosted by DHL – THANK YOU!
  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 20 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content: @Abhishek_Anand32 This is beyond the scope of this blog, since the destination should point to whatever document store you want to use. But I will look into this in the next week and write separately about this.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content:
@Julia_Kraus16 Are you asking or telling me about pipelines, or has there been a change I wasn't aware of?

With this method of creating document grounding, all you need to supply is a destination to an AI Core and to a Document Store. SAP Build (Joule Studio) will handle:

  • Creating the Resource Group
  • Adding the Generic Secret (include all that Base64 stuff)
  • Create a pipeline for the documents in your Document Store

All the stuff you have to do for document grounding in AI Core, still needs to be done but Joule Studio will do it for you. Of course, currently you cannot reuse an existing pipeline you created manually, but I am told that is coming.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content:
@nicholas_nicolis I am sorry I missed your question from a while ago. I am assuming you created a Document Grounding in the Control Tower but your agent is not able to get information from it.

First, it's important to make sure agent is built the best it can be so that it always recognizes when to use the Document Grounding tool you added. 

Second, assuming the first is OK, sometimes Document Grounding has not worked for me. To be honest, I haven't found a good answer for that. I often reload the project, stop and restart the testing, and it will eventually work, but I'm not sure what is going on.

Third, you must consider that something is wrong with AI Core, which has had some periods of instability over the last month.

entry.title: How to remove IAS users from Work Zone, Advanced
entry.content:
I ran Identity Provision, and was able to provision users into Work Zone, Advanced. But I made a mistake and I want to remove certain users. How do I do this?

I tried by filtering the source (and target) to only recognize users whose last name was "Wroblewski", which seems to have worked since in the logs it shows only 2 users read. And I also added the property ips.delete.existedbefore.entities and set to true, and I thought this would remove all but "Wroblewski" users. But it did not work.

Also, can't I add the users to groups in IAS and then just provision the groups into Work Zone?

@Yumnahabbasi @florian_buech

entry.title: Error with Identity Provisioning Tab in Work Zone, advanced edition
entry.content:
I have set up SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition and it is working fine, and I even provisioned users and I am the admin.

But when I open site settings for it, under the Identity Provisioning tab, I see an error and the retry button is disabled.

WHat does this mean? How do I fix it?

entry.title: Re: How to remove IAS users from Work Zone, Advanced
entry.content:
I have a bunch of questions. Is it possible for us to have a short call?

I have only one set of IAS groups but I have many tenants with WZ. But more importantly, the users I want to remove are not in any of those Work Zone groups — I did provisioning and they now appear in WZ advanced. Perhaps I did the provisioning wrong

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content: @KodyZhang Yes. Joule Studio does nothing more than create resource group, generic secret to the document store, and then a pipeline. If you need some sort of orchestration or filtering or whatever, then you have to do it via code. But note that at the moment you cannot import a pipeline created in AICore manually or via code into a Joule Studio project.

entry.title: Re: Request SAP CodeJam Belgium - Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding
entry.content: Thanks, Wouter ... I will reach out to you offline 😊

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content: @erickgomez-tech  No I haven't but if you say your using DMS as storage system and it is working fine, doesn't that mean you have a destination properly configured?
